Illinois · State Licensing Reference

Speech-Language Pathologist
Licensed Speech-Language Pathologist (SLP) Guide

Issued by the Division of Professional Regulation — Speech-Language Pathology under the Illinois Department of Financial and Professional Regulation (IDFPR). Data verified for 2026.

Visit Official Board Portal
Fees Board Contact License Types How to Apply Documents Telepractice Reciprocity FAQ
Total Initial Fee
$90
$90 application fee (by examination)
Renewal
$100
Every 2 years (October 31, odd years)
Processing Time
4 to 6 weeks
Subject to board volume
License Duration
2 years
From date of issuance
Board Contact

Division of Professional Regulation — Speech-Language Pathology

Mailing Address

320 W. Washington St., 3rd Floor, Springfield, IL 62786

Physical Address

320 W. Washington St., 3rd Floor, Springfield, IL 62786

Phone & Fax

Phone: (217) 785-0800

Fax: (217) 782-7645

Email

FPR.COREHelp@illinois.gov

Credential Categories

Illinois SLP License Types

Licensed Speech-Language Pathologist

The full credential to independently practice speech-language pathology in Illinois. Requires a master's degree, completion of the Clinical Fellowship Year (CFY), and a passing Praxis score.

Temporary SLP License

Issued to SLP graduates completing their Clinical Fellowship Year (CFY) or to out-of-state SLPs awaiting Illinois licensure. Application fee: $75. Allows supervised practice while completing remaining requirements.

Application Process

How to Apply for a Illinois Licensed Speech-Language Pathologist (SLP)

Earn a Master's Degree in SLP

Complete a master's or doctoral degree in speech pathology from an IDFPR-approved and/or ASHA-accredited program.

Pass the Praxis Examination

Achieve a passing score on the Praxis Examination in Speech-Language Pathology (test code 5331). The current passing score is 162.

Complete the Clinical Fellowship Year (CFY)

Complete a supervised Clinical Fellowship Year under a licensed SLP. A Temporary SLP License ($75) must be obtained first to practice during the CFY in Illinois.

Apply through IDFPR CORE Portal

Submit the application through the IDFPR CORE online licensing system. Pay the $90 application fee. Include official transcripts, Praxis scores, CFY verification, and background check.

Required Documents

Application Document Checklist

  • Official graduate transcriptsFrom an IDFPR-approved or ASHA-accredited speech-language pathology program.
  • Praxis exam score reportScore of 162 or higher on test 5331.
  • Clinical Fellowship verificationDocumentation of completed CFY signed by supervising licensed SLP.
  • Background checkCriminal background check as required by IDFPR.
Telepractice

Illinois Telepractice Rules for SLPs

Telehealth Practice

Illinois permits telepractice for licensed SLPs. The practitioner must hold a valid Illinois SLP license to provide services to clients located in Illinois. Illinois does not currently participate in the ASLP Interstate Compact. All telepractice services must meet the same standard of care as in-person services.

Practice Laws

Provisional Practice & Reciprocity

Clinical Fellowship & Provisional Rules

Illinois issues a Temporary SLP License for graduates completing their Clinical Fellowship Year (CFY). The temporary license costs $75 and permits supervised practice while the CFY is being completed. Out-of-state SLPs awaiting Illinois licensure may also apply for the temporary license.

Reciprocity & Endorsement

Illinois offers licensure by endorsement for SLPs licensed in other states. The endorsement application fee is $100. Applicants must demonstrate equivalent education, examination, and experience. ASHA CCC-SLP holders may have the review process streamlined but must still submit a full Illinois application.

Frequently Asked Questions

Illinois SLP FAQ

How much does an Illinois SLP license cost?

The initial application fee is $90 (by examination) or $100 (by endorsement). A Temporary SLP License costs $75. Biennial renewal is $100, due by October 31 of odd-numbered years.

Do I need a PEL to work as an SLP in Illinois schools?

Yes. SLPs working in Illinois public schools must hold a Professional Educator License (PEL) from the Illinois State Board of Education in addition to the IDFPR professional SLP license.

What continuing education is required for Illinois SLP renewal?

Illinois requires 20 hours of CE every two years, including 1 hour of ethics, 1 hour of sexual harassment training, 1 hour of implicit bias awareness, 1 hour of dementia recognition training (every 6 years), and 1 hour of cultural competency training (effective January 1, 2025).
